manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: grass, sock, satellite, ketchup, code, pyramid, sink, police, boot.
Your opponent's team words are: moscow, capital, pound, tap, phoenix, ruler, saturn, deck.
Distractor words are: field, fish, germany, eye, whale, duck, shoe.
The kill words are: jam.

message.content:
CLUE: track
TARGETS: police, satellite, code, boot
message.reasoning:
We need to output clue and target list. Choose a clue that relates to some of our team's words. Must be single word. Must not be any of the words in the lists, nor contain parts of them.

Our team's words: grass, sock, satellite, ketchup, code, pyramid, sink (maybe sink? Actually sink is a team word), police, boot.

We need clue that relates to several of these. Perhaps "red" relates to ketchup (red), police? maybe not. "Foot" relates to sock, boot, police? maybe foot patrol. "Space" relates to satellite, pyramid (Egypt?), sink? Not.

Maybe "blue" relates to police (blue uniforms) and sink (blue water) and grass (green). Not clear.

Goal to maximize words.

Clue "log" relates to code (log), boot (log boots? not). Hmm.

Clue "water" relates to sink, grass (needs water), satellite? Not.

Clue "Egypt" relates to pyramid, maybe could link to "code" (Egyptian code?), not.

Clue "circuit" relates to code, satellite, police (radio), maybe not.

Clue "foot" seems good: sock, boot, police (foot patrol), maybe "grass" (foot grass? foot as in foot of a hill). Could also relate to "sink" (foot sink?). Not.

Better clue: "yard" relates to grass, police (yard? police yard), boot (boot yard?), sink? No.

Clue "network" relates to satellite, code, police (network). Not strong.

Clue "red" connects to ketchup (red), police (red lights? no), boot (red boot). maybe not.

Need to avoid kill word "jam". Can't clue "jam" because it's kill.

Clue must not contain parts of any words. For example "boot" can't be used. "sock" can't. So choose something else.

Maybe clue "digital". Relates to code, satellite (digital transmission), police (digital forensics), maybe not.

Better to pick "clean". Relates to sink (clean dishes), police (clean record), boot (clean boots), but not strongly.

Maybe "command". Relates to code, police command, boot command (boot up), satellite command? Might be plausible.

We need a single-word clue that is clearly semantically linked. Could be "bootcamp". No parts "boot". That's okay but "bootcamp" contains "boot". Actually cannot contain parts of them. "bootcamp" includes "boot". So not allowed.

Clue "security". Relates to police, code (security code), satellite (security satellite), boot (boot security?). Maybe not.

Clue "track". Relates to police (track criminals), code (tracking code), satellite (tracking), boot (track boots), grass (track on grass). Could be many.

But "track" is a word itself, not a part of any target words. Good.

Thus we can list target words that relate: police, satellite, code, boot, grass? Might be okay. But we must be confident partner can guess. Might be too many.

We could choose a clue that relates to maybe 3-4 words, safe.

Let's pick "track". Related to police (track criminals), satellite (track objects), code (tracking code), boot (track boots used for walking). Possibly also "grass" (track on grass). Great.

But we need to list the target words we intend to get. Could list: police, satellite, code, boot

That's 4. Provide.

Thus output:

CLUE: track
TARGETS: police, satellite, code, boot

Make sure formatting exactly.

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide a comma-separated list of up to 4 words from the following list that best relate or are most closely associated with the word 'track'. Always start your list of guess(es) with 'GUESS: ' and do not include any other text in your answer.

duck, jam, ketchup, fish, satellite, tap, capital, eye, sink, field, ruler, moscow, whale, germany, grass, boot, shoe, phoenix, sock, police, pound, code, pyramid, deck, saturn

message.reasoning:
The user wants a comma-separated list of up to 4 words from the given list that best relate or are most closely associated with the word 'track'. Must start with 'GUESS: ' and no other text.

We need to think which words relate to "track". Could be "field" (as in track field), "rail" but not present. "satellite" (tracking), "code" (track code?), "tap" maybe track? "pound" not. "shoe" (track shoes), "boot". "sink". "duck". Possibly "track and field". So "field" is a good one. "satellite" tracks things. "code" could be tracking code. "pound"? Not. "boot" - track boot? maybe "track boot". "shoe" also. Choose up to 4. Let's pick "field, satellite, shoe, code". Provide as comma-separated after GUESS:.

Check guidelines: Must not include any other text. So answer: GUESS: field, satellite, shoe
